title: DO NOT MERGE dump some networking information around system-tests
body: Trying to figure out what is going on in #1354 .

comments.body:
Succeeded with 
=== ip addr
1: lo:  mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0:  mtu 1460 qdisc mq state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 42:01:0a:80:00:0b brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    altname enp0s4
    altname ens4
    inet 10.128.0.11/32 scope global dynamic noprefixroute eth0
       valid_lft 85602sec preferred_lft 85602sec
    inet6 fe80::4001:aff:fe80:b/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
4: cni-podman0:  mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 0e:5f:9f:f2:08:fe brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.1/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global cni-podman0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::c5f:9fff:fef2:8fe/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
=== ip rule
0:	from all lookup local
32766:	from all lookup main
32767:	from all lookup default
=== ip route list table local
broadcast 10.88.0.0 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
local 10.88.0.1 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope host src 10.88.0.1 
broadcast 10.88.255.255 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
local 10.128.0.11 dev eth0 proto kernel scope host src 10.128.0.11 
broadcast 127.0.0.0 dev lo proto kernel scope link src 127.0.0.1 
local 127.0.0.0/8 dev lo proto kernel scope host src 127.0.0.1 
local 127.0.0.1 dev lo proto kernel scope host src 127.0.0.1 
broadcast 127.255.255.255 dev lo proto kernel scope link src 127.0.0.1 
=== ip route list table main
default via 10.128.0.1 dev eth0 proto dhcp metric 100 
10.88.0.0/16 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
10.128.0.1 dev eth0 proto dhcp scope link metric 100 
=== ip route list table default
Error: ipv4: FIB table does not exist.
Dump terminated
=== ip route get 127.0.0.1
local 127.0.0.1 dev lo src 127.0.0.1 uid 0 
    cache  
===
… and now what?

Failure: 
=== ip addr
1: lo:  mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0:  mtu 1460 qdisc mq state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 42:01:0a:80:0f:d6 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    altname enp0s4
    altname ens4
    inet 10.128.15.214/32 scope global dynamic noprefixroute eth0
       valid_lft 85644sec preferred_lft 85644sec
    inet6 fe80::4001:aff:fe80:fd6/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
4: cni-podman0:  mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state DOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 8e:a0:53:17:04:20 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.1/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global cni-podman0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::8ca0:53ff:fe17:420/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
=== ip rule
0:	from all lookup local
32766:	from all lookup main
32767:	from all lookup default
=== ip route list table local
broadcast 10.88.0.0 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
local 10.88.0.1 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope host src 10.88.0.1 
broadcast 10.88.255.255 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
local 10.128.15.214 dev eth0 proto kernel scope host src 10.128.15.214 
broadcast 127.0.0.0 dev lo proto kernel scope link src 127.0.0.1 
local 127.0.0.0/8 dev lo proto kernel scope host src 127.0.0.1 
local 127.0.0.1 dev lo proto kernel scope host src 127.0.0.1 
broadcast 127.255.255.255 dev lo proto kernel scope link src 127.0.0.1 
=== ip route list table main
default via 10.128.0.1 dev eth0 proto dhcp metric 100 
10.88.0.0/16 dev cni-podman0 proto kernel scope link src 10.88.0.1 linkdown 
10.128.0.1 dev eth0 proto dhcp scope link metric 100 
=== ip route list table default
Error: ipv4: FIB table does not exist.
Dump terminated
=== ip route get 127.0.0.1
local 127.0.0.1 dev lo src 127.0.0.1 uid 0 
    cache  
===
This data looks inconclusive, anyway it has been captured and recorded above, so we don’t need to keep this PR around.
